test_cases.description: main.go calls cli.Execute() and passes the returned error to os.Exit(cli.ExitCode(err)), producing no output itself.
preconditions: cmd/akc/main.go exists
internal/cli package is compiled
test_cases.input: Execute() returns a non-nil error of type plain error
test_cases.expected: os.Exit is called with the integer returned by ExitCode(err); no bytes written to stdout or stderr by main.go itself
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout/stderr; invoke main(); assert stdout contains only what Execute() wrote; assert main.go added no additional bytes; assert os.Exit was called with ExitCode(err)

test_cases.description: Execute() calls PrintError(err) on any non-nil error returned by rootCmd.Execute() before returning that error.
preconditions: internal/cli package initialised with a stub RunE that returns a plain error
test_cases.input: rootCmd.Execute() returns a non-nil error
test_cases.expected: stdout contains exactly one JSON error envelope; Execute() returns the same non-nil error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: buf = captureStdout(); err = Execute(); assert err != nil; assert buf contains '{"error":{'; assert buf has exactly one occurrence of '{"error":'

test_cases.description: Root command is configured with SilenceErrors and SilenceUsage so Cobra never prints its own error or usage text.
preconditions: internal/cli root command initialised
test_cases.input: inspect rootCmd fields after initialisation
test_cases.expected: rootCmd.SilenceErrors == true and rootCmd.SilenceUsage == true
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: rootCmd = buildRootCommand(); assert rootCmd.SilenceErrors == true; assert rootCmd.SilenceUsage == true

test_cases.description: Individual command RunE calls PrintJSON on success and returns nil without calling PrintError.
preconditions: a stub command with RunE that calls PrintJSON and returns nil is registered
test_cases.input: invoke the stub command successfully
test_cases.expected: stdout contains valid JSON result; stderr is empty; no error envelope written
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout, stderr = captureOutputs(); err = invokeStubCommand(); assert err == nil; assert isValidJSON(stdout); assert not contains(stdout, '"error"'); assert stderr == ''

test_cases.description: On failure, RunE returns the error to Execute() which calls PrintError centrally, producing exactly one envelope.
preconditions: a stub command with RunE that returns a plain error is registered
test_cases.input: invoke the stub command that returns an error
test_cases.expected: stdout contains exactly one JSON error envelope; no duplicate envelopes
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout = captureStdout(); Execute(); count = occurrences(stdout, '"error":'); assert count == 1

test_cases.description: Root command defines --endpoint-url, --user-id, --api-key, and --json as persistent flags available to all subcommands.
preconditions: internal/cli root command initialised
test_cases.input: inspect root command's persistent flag set
test_cases.expected: PersistentFlags() contains flags named 'endpoint-url' (string), 'user-id' (string), 'api-key' (string), 'json' (bool)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: flags = rootCmd.PersistentFlags(); assert flags.Lookup('endpoint-url').Value.Type() == 'string'; assert flags.Lookup('user-id').Value.Type() == 'string'; assert flags.Lookup('api-key').Value.Type() == 'string'; assert flags.Lookup('json').Value.Type() == 'bool'

test_cases.description: Invoking the root command with no subcommand and no flags causes Cobra to print help text to stdout and exit 0.
preconditions: internal/cli root command initialised with no RunE
test_cases.input: Execute() called with args = []
test_cases.expected: stdout contains help text; exit code 0; stderr is empty
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout = captureStdout(); err = Execute(); assert err == nil; assert len(stdout) > 0; assert contains(stdout, 'Usage') or contains(stdout, 'akc')

test_cases.description: Non-help commands silently ignore the --json persistent flag, producing identical output with or without it.
preconditions: version command registered and annotated with 'auth': 'none'
no endpoint configured
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc version' once with --json and once without
test_cases.expected: both invocations produce identical stdout content; no warning or error emitted for the --json flag
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: out1 = captureStdout(); Execute(['version']); out2 = captureStdout(); Execute(['version', '--json']); assert out1 == out2

test_cases.description: Root apikit package exports RootCommand() as a shim delegating to internal/cli.RootCommand().
preconditions: root apikit package compiled
test_cases.input: call apikit.RootCommand()
test_cases.expected: returned *cobra.Command is non-nil; its Use field matches the CLI binary name; no internal/cli import required by caller
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cmd = apikit.RootCommand(); assert cmd != nil; assert cmd.Use contains 'akc'

test_cases.description: version_info.go declares Version, Build, and TokenPrefix with correct default values.
preconditions: internal/cli package compiled without -ldflags overrides
test_cases.input: read package-level variables Version, Build, TokenPrefix
test_cases.expected: Version == 'dev'; Build == 'unknown'; TokenPrefix == 'ak'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert cli.Version == 'dev'; assert cli.Build == 'unknown'; assert cli.TokenPrefix == 'ak'

test_cases.description: PersistentPreRunE returns exit-2 error with the exact message when TokenPrefix is empty, before any config init.
preconditions: TokenPrefix set to empty string in test
a non-auth-exempt command is registered
test_cases.input: invoke any non-auth-exempt command with TokenPrefix = ''
test_cases.expected: stdout contains JSON envelope with code:0 and message 'TokenPrefix is empty: binary was built without a valid -ldflags TokenPrefix value'; exit code 2
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cli.TokenPrefix = ''; stdout = captureStdout(); err = Execute(['someCommand']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.error.code == 0; assert env.error.message == 'TokenPrefix is empty: binary was built without a valid -ldflags TokenPrefix value'; assert ExitCode(err) == 2

test_cases.description: InitConfig creates the config directory with mode 0700 when it does not exist.
preconditions: temp directory created as HOME; config directory does not exist
test_cases.input: call InitConfig(configDir) where configDir does not exist
test_cases.expected: directory created at configDir with permission bits 0700
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: tmpHome = createTempDir(); configDir = tmpHome + '/.ak'; InitConfig(configDir); info = stat(configDir); assert info.IsDir() == true; assert info.Mode().Perm() == 0700

test_cases.description: InitConfig creates config.toml from the hard-coded template with mode 0600 when it does not exist.
preconditions: config directory exists; config.toml does not exist
test_cases.input: call InitConfig(configDir)
test_cases.expected: config.toml created with mode 0600; file contents exactly match the three-line template
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: InitConfig(configDir); info = stat(configDir+'/config.toml'); assert info.Mode().Perm() == 0600; content = readFile(configDir+'/config.toml'); assert contains(content, 'endpoint_url = ""'); assert contains(content, 'user_id = ""'); assert contains(content, 'api_key = ""')

test_cases.description: InitConfig does not modify an existing config.toml.
preconditions: config directory and config.toml exist with custom content
test_cases.input: call InitConfig(configDir) when config.toml already has custom content
test_cases.expected: config.toml content and permissions remain unchanged after InitConfig
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
originalContent = 'endpoint_url = "http://example.com"
user_id = "u1"
api_key = "k1"
'; writeFile(configPath, originalContent); InitConfig(configDir); assert readFile(configPath) == originalContent

test_cases.description: CLIConfig struct has three string fields with correct TOML tags.
preconditions: internal/cli package compiled
test_cases.input: inspect CLIConfig struct via reflection or TOML round-trip
test_cases.expected: EndpointURL tagged 'endpoint_url', UserID tagged 'user_id', APIKey tagged 'api_key'; all fields are strings
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cfg = CLIConfig{EndpointURL: 'e', UserID: 'u', APIKey: 'k'}; encoded = tomlEncode(cfg); assert contains(encoded, 'endpoint_url = "e"'); assert contains(encoded, 'user_id = "u"'); assert contains(encoded, 'api_key = "k"')

test_cases.description: LoadConfig parses a valid config.toml into CLIConfig with all three fields populated.
preconditions: config.toml exists with all three fields set to non-empty values
test_cases.input: call LoadConfig(configDir) with a valid TOML file containing endpoint_url, user_id, api_key
test_cases.expected: returns (*CLIConfig, nil) with EndpointURL, UserID, APIKey matching file values
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
writeFile(configPath, 'endpoint_url = "http://x"
user_id = "u"
api_key = "k"
'); cfg, err = LoadConfig(configDir); assert err == nil; assert cfg.EndpointURL == 'http://x'; assert cfg.UserID == 'u'; assert cfg.APIKey == 'k'

test_cases.description: LoadConfig rejects any TOML parse error entirely, returning (nil, error) with the canonical message prefix.
preconditions: config.toml exists with syntactically invalid TOML content
test_cases.input: call LoadConfig(configDir) with a malformed config.toml
test_cases.expected: returns (nil, error) where error.Error() starts with 'config file is unparseable:'; no partial CLIConfig is returned
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeFile(configPath, 'endpoint_url = !!!INVALID!!!'); cfg, err = LoadConfig(configDir); assert cfg == nil; assert err != nil; assert startsWith(err.Error(), 'config file is unparseable:')

test_cases.description: ResolveField returns the flag value when flagChanged is true, regardless of env or config.
preconditions: env var set to a different value; config has yet another value
test_cases.input: call ResolveField('endpoint_url', '--endpoint-url', 'flagVal', true, 'ENDPOINT_URL', 'configVal', true)
test_cases.expected: returns ('flagVal', nil)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: setEnv('ENDPOINT_URL', 'envVal'); val, err = ResolveField('endpoint_url', '--endpoint-url', 'flagVal', true, 'ENDPOINT_URL', 'configVal', true); assert err == nil; assert val == 'flagVal'

test_cases.description: ResolveField returns the env var value when flag is not changed and env is non-empty.
preconditions: ENDPOINT_URL env var set to 'envVal'; config has 'configVal'
test_cases.input: call ResolveField('endpoint_url', '--endpoint-url', '', false, 'ENDPOINT_URL', 'configVal', true)
test_cases.expected: returns ('envVal', nil)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: setEnv('ENDPOINT_URL', 'envVal'); val, err = ResolveField('endpoint_url', '--endpoint-url', '', false, 'ENDPOINT_URL', 'configVal', true); assert err == nil; assert val == 'envVal'

test_cases.description: ResolveField returns the config value when flag is not changed and env is empty.
preconditions: ENDPOINT_URL env var is empty or unset
test_cases.input: call ResolveField('endpoint_url', '--endpoint-url', '', false, 'ENDPOINT_URL', 'configVal', true)
test_cases.expected: returns ('configVal', nil)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: unsetEnv('ENDPOINT_URL'); val, err = ResolveField('endpoint_url', '--endpoint-url', '', false, 'ENDPOINT_URL', 'configVal', true); assert err == nil; assert val == 'configVal'

test_cases.description: ResolveField returns canonical error when required=true and all sources are unset.
preconditions: ENDPOINT_URL env var is unset; configValue is empty string
test_cases.input: call ResolveField('endpoint_url', '--endpoint-url', '', false, 'ENDPOINT_URL', '', true)
test_cases.expected: returns ('', error) where error.Error() == 'endpoint_url is not set: provide via --endpoint-url, $ENDPOINT_URL, or config file'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: unsetEnv('ENDPOINT_URL'); val, err = ResolveField('endpoint_url', '--endpoint-url', '', false, 'ENDPOINT_URL', '', true); assert val == ''; assert err != nil; assert err.Error() == 'endpoint_url is not set: provide via --endpoint-url, $ENDPOINT_URL, or config file'

test_cases.description: ResolveField returns ('', nil) when required=false and all sources are unset.
preconditions: USER_ID env var is unset; configValue is empty string
test_cases.input: call ResolveField('user_id', '--user-id', '', false, 'USER_ID', '', false)
test_cases.expected: returns ('', nil) — no error for optional missing field
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: unsetEnv('USER_ID'); val, err = ResolveField('user_id', '--user-id', '', false, 'USER_ID', '', false); assert val == ''; assert err == nil

test_cases.description: ResolveField composes the canonical error message internally from fieldName, flagName, and envVarName parameters.
preconditions: all sources unset; required=true
test_cases.input: call ResolveField('api_key', '--api-key', '', false, 'API_KEY', '', true)
test_cases.expected: error message is exactly 'api_key is not set: provide via --api-key, $API_KEY, or config file'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: _, err = ResolveField('api_key', '--api-key', '', false, 'API_KEY', '', true); assert err.Error() == 'api_key is not set: provide via --api-key, $API_KEY, or config file'

test_cases.description: PersistentPreRunE resolves endpoint_url and api_key as required, and user_id as optional (empty string stored in context without error).
preconditions: endpoint_url and api_key provided via env; user_id unset in all sources; non-auth-exempt command registered; InitConfig has run
test_cases.input: invoke a non-auth-exempt command with ENDPOINT_URL and API_KEY env vars set, USER_ID unset
test_cases.expected: command RunE runs successfully; UserIDFromContext returns empty string; ClientFromContext returns non-nil client
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: setEnv('ENDPOINT_URL', 'http://localhost'); setEnv('API_KEY', 'testkey'); unsetEnv('USER_ID'); err = Execute(['testCmd']); assert err == nil; assert ClientFromContext(ctx) != nil; assert UserIDFromContext(ctx) == ''

test_cases.description: PersistentPreRunE checks flag presence using cmd.Flags().Changed() on the leaf command, not the root.
preconditions: non-auth-exempt leaf command registered; endpoint_url set via flag
test_cases.input: invoke leaf command with --endpoint-url flag explicitly set
test_cases.expected: ResolveField receives flagChanged=true and uses the flag value; credential resolved from flag source
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout = captureStdout(); Execute(['leafCmd', '--endpoint-url', 'http://flag-val', '--api-key', 'k']); verifyResolvedEndpointURL == 'http://flag-val'

test_cases.description: ResolveEndpointURL returns empty string (not an error) when no endpoint is configured in any source.
preconditions: ENDPOINT_URL env var unset; config.toml has empty endpoint_url; --endpoint-url flag not set
test_cases.input: call ResolveEndpointURL(cmd) with no endpoint configured anywhere
test_cases.expected: returns empty string; no error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: unsetEnv('ENDPOINT_URL'); writeConfig(configDir, CLIConfig{}); val = ResolveEndpointURL(cmd); assert val == ''

test_cases.description: context.go defines unexported zero-size struct types clientContextKey and userIDContextKey as context keys.
preconditions: internal/cli package compiled
test_cases.input: inspect type definitions in context.go via source or reflection
test_cases.expected: clientContextKey and userIDContextKey are unexported struct types; they are zero-size structs
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: // verified via compilation: no external package can construct clientContextKey{} or userIDContextKey{}; assert sizeof(clientContextKey{}) == 0; assert sizeof(userIDContextKey{}) == 0

test_cases.description: ClientFromContext returns nil when called with a plain context that has no stored client.
preconditions: plain context.Background() used
test_cases.input: call ClientFromContext(context.Background())
test_cases.expected: returns nil; no panic
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: client = ClientFromContext(context.Background()); assert client == nil

test_cases.description: ClientFromContext returns the stored *apikit.Client when one has been stored via PersistentPreRunE.
preconditions: context populated with a client via context.WithValue using clientContextKey{}
test_cases.input: call ClientFromContext(ctx) after storing a client
test_cases.expected: returns the exact same *apikit.Client that was stored
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockClient = newMockClient(); ctx = context.WithValue(context.Background(), clientContextKey{}, mockClient); result = ClientFromContext(ctx); assert result == mockClient

test_cases.description: UserIDFromContext returns empty string when called with a plain context.
preconditions: plain context.Background() used
test_cases.input: call UserIDFromContext(context.Background())
test_cases.expected: returns empty string; no panic
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: uid = UserIDFromContext(context.Background()); assert uid == ''

test_cases.description: UserIDFromContext returns the stored user_id string when set in context.
preconditions: context populated with user_id via context.WithValue using userIDContextKey{}
test_cases.input: call UserIDFromContext(ctx) after storing 'user-123'
test_cases.expected: returns 'user-123'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: ctx = context.WithValue(context.Background(), userIDContextKey{}, 'user-123'); uid = UserIDFromContext(ctx); assert uid == 'user-123'

test_cases.description: PersistentPreRunE stores *apikit.Client and user_id in context via context.WithValue; user_id is not passed to apikit.NewClient.
preconditions: all credentials configured; non-auth-exempt command registered
test_cases.input: invoke non-auth-exempt command with endpoint_url, api_key, user_id all configured
test_cases.expected: ClientFromContext returns non-nil client; UserIDFromContext returns the resolved user_id; apikit.NewClient was called with only endpoint_url and api_key
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: setEnv('ENDPOINT_URL', 'http://server'); setEnv('API_KEY', 'k'); setEnv('USER_ID', 'u123'); capturedCtx = nil; registerRunE(func(cmd, args){ capturedCtx = cmd.Context() }); Execute(['cmd']); assert ClientFromContext(capturedCtx) != nil; assert UserIDFromContext(capturedCtx) == 'u123'

test_cases.description: SaveConfig atomically writes CLIConfig to config.toml using a temp file and os.Rename; file has mode 0600.
preconditions: config directory exists and is writable; config.toml may or may not exist
test_cases.input: call SaveConfig(configDir, &CLIConfig{EndpointURL: 'http://x', UserID: 'u', APIKey: 'k'})
test_cases.expected: config.toml exists with mode 0600; content is valid TOML with all three fields; no temp files remain in configDir
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: SaveConfig(configDir, cfg); info = stat(configDir+'/config.toml'); assert info.Mode().Perm() == 0600; content = readFile(configDir+'/config.toml'); parsed = tomlDecode(content); assert parsed.EndpointURL == 'http://x'; tempFiles = listDir(configDir); assert no file in tempFiles matches 'tmp*'

test_cases.description: SaveConfig returns error immediately when os.CreateTemp fails; no temp file or deferred remove registered.
preconditions: config directory made unwritable (chmod 000)
test_cases.input: call SaveConfig(configDir, cfg) with unwritable config directory
test_cases.expected: error returned; no temp file left in configDir; original config.toml unchanged
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: chmod(configDir, 0000); err = SaveConfig(configDir, cfg); assert err != nil; chmod(configDir, 0700); tempFiles = listDir(configDir); assert no file in tempFiles matches 'tmp*'

test_cases.description: When os.Rename fails after temp file is written, deferred os.Remove cleans up the temp file and original config.toml remains untouched.
preconditions: valid config.toml exists; os.Rename will be made to fail by making target directory read-only after os.CreateTemp succeeds
test_cases.input: call SaveConfig with injected Rename failure
test_cases.expected: error returned; no temp file remains; original config.toml content is unchanged
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: originalContent = readFile(configPath); injectRenameFailure(); err = SaveConfig(configDir, newCfg); assert err != nil; assert readFile(configPath) == originalContent; tempFiles = listDir(configDir); assert no temp file remains

test_cases.description: SaveConfig creates the temp file in the same directory as config.toml to guarantee same-filesystem rename.
preconditions: config directory exists
test_cases.input: call SaveConfig(configDir, cfg) and observe where temp file is created
test_cases.expected: temp file is created inside configDir before rename; os.Rename source and destination are on the same filesystem
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: interceptCreateTemp = true; SaveConfig(configDir, cfg); assert capturedTempDir == configDir

test_cases.description: PrintJSON marshals the value with json.MarshalIndent (two-space indent) and writes it to stdout.
preconditions: stdout is captured
test_cases.input: call PrintJSON(map[string]string{'key': 'value'})
test_cases.expected: stdout contains '{\n  "key": "value"\n}'; output is valid JSON; no error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: buf = captureStdout(); err = PrintJSON(map{'key': 'value'}); assert err == nil; assert buf == '{\n  "key": "value"\n}'

test_cases.description: PrintError writes JSON error envelope to stdout only with correct code and message; nothing written to stderr.
preconditions: stdout and stderr are captured
test_cases.input: call PrintError with a plain error 'some client error'
test_cases.expected: stdout == '{"error":{"code":0,"message":"some client error"}}'; stderr is empty
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout, stderr = captureOutputs(); PrintError(errors.New('some client error')); assert parseJSON(stdout).error.code == 0; assert parseJSON(stdout).error.message == 'some client error'; assert stderr == ''

test_cases.description: PrintError with *apikit.APIError uses the error's HTTP status code (>= 400) and message; code is never 0 for API errors.
preconditions: stdout captured; apiErr with Code=404, Message='not found' constructed
test_cases.input: call PrintError(apiErr) where apiErr is *apikit.APIError{Code: 404, Message: 'not found'}
test_cases.expected: stdout envelope has code=404 and message='not found'; code != 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: apiErr = newAPIError(404, 'not found'); stdout = captureStdout(); PrintError(apiErr); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.error.code == 404; assert env.error.message == 'not found'; assert env.error.code != 0

test_cases.description: ExitCode returns 0 for nil, 1 for *apikit.APIError, and 2 for all other non-nil errors.
preconditions: internal/cli package compiled
test_cases.input: call ExitCode with nil, *apikit.APIError, and plain error respectively
test_cases.expected: returns 0, 1, 2 in that order
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert ExitCode(nil) == 0; assert ExitCode(newAPIError(401, 'unauthorized')) == 1; assert ExitCode(errors.New('some error')) == 2

test_cases.description: Human-readable messages (warnings, progress) are written to stderr only and never appear on stdout.
preconditions: stdout and stderr captured; a command that prints a warning is invoked
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc version' with endpoint configured but server unreachable
test_cases.expected: stdout contains only JSON; stderr contains the warning text; warning text not present on stdout
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout, stderr = captureOutputs(); Execute(['version']); assert isValidJSON(stdout); assert not contains(stdout, 'warning'); assert contains(stderr, 'warning')

test_cases.description: The version command has 'auth': 'none' and 'composite': 'true' annotations, skipping PersistentPreRunE client creation.
preconditions: internal/cli version command initialised
test_cases.input: inspect version command's Annotations map
test_cases.expected: Annotations['auth'] == 'none' and Annotations['composite'] == 'true'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: versionCmd = findCommand(rootCmd, 'version'); assert versionCmd.Annotations['auth'] == 'none'; assert versionCmd.Annotations['composite'] == 'true'

test_cases.description: VersionOutput struct has correct fields and JSON tags; ServerVersion uses *apikit.VersionResponse with omitempty.
preconditions: internal/cli package compiled
test_cases.input: marshal VersionOutput with nil ServerVersion
test_cases.expected: JSON has cli_version, build, prefix fields; server_version is omitted entirely (not null)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: out = VersionOutput{CLIVersion: '1.0', Build: 'abc', Prefix: 'ak', ServerVersion: nil}; json = marshal(out); assert contains(json, '"cli_version"'); assert contains(json, '"build"'); assert contains(json, '"prefix"'); assert not contains(json, 'server_version')

test_cases.description: Version command RunE constructs an apikit.Client and calls client.Version(ctx) when ResolveEndpointURL returns non-empty URL.
preconditions: ENDPOINT_URL set to a mock server URL; mock server returns VersionResponse
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc version' with endpoint configured and mock server reachable
test_cases.expected: stdout JSON has server_version object with version, build_time, commit, mount_point fields
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer = startMockServer(versionResponse); setEnv('ENDPOINT_URL', mockServer.URL); stdout = captureStdout(); Execute(['version']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.server_version != nil; assert env.server_version.version != ''; assert env.server_version.build_time != ''; assert env.server_version.commit != ''; assert env.server_version.mount_point != ''

test_cases.description: Version command omits server_version and prints no warning when ResolveEndpointURL returns empty string.
preconditions: ENDPOINT_URL unset; --endpoint-url flag not passed; config.toml has empty endpoint_url
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc version' with no endpoint configured anywhere
test_cases.expected: stdout JSON has cli_version, build, prefix only; no server_version key; stderr is empty
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: unsetEnv('ENDPOINT_URL'); stdout, stderr = captureOutputs(); Execute(['version']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.cli_version != nil; assert not hasKey(env, 'server_version'); assert stderr == ''

test_cases.description: Version command omits server_version and prints warning to stderr when endpoint is configured but server is unreachable.
preconditions: ENDPOINT_URL set to non-listening address; no server running at that address
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc version' with endpoint configured but server unreachable
test_cases.expected: stdout JSON has cli_version, build, prefix; no server_version; stderr contains 'warning: could not reach server:'; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: setEnv('ENDPOINT_URL', 'http://127.0.0.1:19999'); stdout, stderr = captureOutputs(); err = Execute(['version']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert not hasKey(env, 'server_version'); assert contains(stderr, 'warning: could not reach server:'); assert ExitCode(err) == 0

test_cases.description: Custom help subcommand replaces Cobra's default; outputs full JSON tree when --json is set; delegates to Cobra default when --json is not set.
preconditions: internal/cli command tree initialised with stub commands
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc help --json' and separately 'akc help'
test_cases.expected: first invocation: stdout is valid JSON with 'commands' array; second invocation: stdout is standard Cobra help text (not JSON)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout1 = captureStdout(); Execute(['help', '--json']); assert isValidJSON(stdout1); assert hasKey(parseJSON(stdout1), 'commands'); stdout2 = captureStdout(); Execute(['help']); assert not isValidJSON(stdout2)

test_cases.description: help --json output root object has name, version, and commands array; each command entry has required fields.
preconditions: stub leaf commands registered with Annotations
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc help --json'
test_cases.expected: root object has 'name', 'version', 'commands'; each command entry has 'name', 'description', 'method', 'path', 'args', 'flags', 'auth'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout = captureStdout(); Execute(['help', '--json']); tree = parseJSON(stdout); assert tree.name != ''; assert tree.version != ''; assert isArray(tree.commands); for cmd in tree.commands: assert hasKey(cmd, 'name'); assert hasKey(cmd, 'description'); assert hasKey(cmd, 'method'); assert hasKey(cmd, 'path'); assert hasKey(cmd, 'args'); assert hasKey(cmd, 'flags'); assert hasKey(cmd, 'auth')

test_cases.description: help --json walker collects only leaf commands (RunE set + at least one Annotations key); excludes group commands without RunE.
preconditions: group command 'user' without RunE registered; leaf command 'user show' with RunE and Annotations registered
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc help --json'
test_cases.expected: commands array contains 'user show' but not bare 'user' group command
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout = captureStdout(); Execute(['help', '--json']); cmds = parseJSON(stdout).commands; names = [c.name for c in cmds]; assert 'user show' in names; assert 'user' not in names

test_cases.description: Flag descriptors omit default for zero-value defaults; include default for non-zero defaults; rule applies to all types including bool.
preconditions: a leaf command with bool flag (default false), string flag (default ''), int flag (default 0), and bool flag (default true) registered
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc help --json' and inspect flag descriptors
test_cases.expected: bool(false) descriptor has no 'default' key; string('') descriptor has no 'default' key; int(0) descriptor has no 'default' key; bool(true) descriptor has 'default': true
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: Execute(['help', '--json']); cmd = findCommandInOutput('leafCmd'); flags = cmd.flags; boolFalseFlag = findFlag(flags, 'myflag'); assert not hasKey(boolFalseFlag, 'default'); boolTrueFlag = findFlag(flags, 'verbose'); assert boolTrueFlag.default == true

test_cases.description: Custom SetHelpFunc outputs single command JSON when --json is set; falls back to Cobra default help without --json.
preconditions: version command registered with Annotations
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc version --help --json' and separately 'akc version --help'
test_cases.expected: first: stdout is JSON for version command entry only; second: stdout is standard Cobra help text
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout1 = captureStdout(); Execute(['version', '--help', '--json']); entry = parseJSON(stdout1); assert entry.name == 'version'; stdout2 = captureStdout(); Execute(['version', '--help']); assert not isValidJSON(stdout2) or not hasKey(parseJSON(stdout2), 'name')

test_cases.description: help --json walker excludes commands with no keys in Annotations (Cobra built-ins); only annotation-carrying commands appear.
preconditions: an unannotated command (simulating Cobra built-in) is present in the tree
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc help --json'
test_cases.expected: unannotated command does not appear in commands array; all appearing commands have at least one Annotations key
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: addUnannotatedCommand(rootCmd, 'builtinCmd'); Execute(['help', '--json']); cmds = parseJSON(stdout).commands; for cmd in cmds: assert cmdHasAnnotations(cmd); assert 'builtinCmd' not in [c.name for c in cmds]

test_cases.description: Commands added by consuming projects via AddCommand with at least the 'auth' annotation automatically appear in help --json output.
preconditions: consuming project command with 'auth': 'api_key' annotation added via AddCommand
test_cases.input: invoke 'akc help --json'
test_cases.expected: consuming project command appears in commands array without any additional registration step
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: thirdPartyCmd = newCommand('myapp do', RunE, Annotations{'auth': 'api_key'}); rootCmd.AddCommand(thirdPartyCmd); stdout = captureStdout(); Execute(['help', '--json']); cmds = parseJSON(stdout).commands; assert 'myapp do' in [c.name for c in cmds]

test_cases.description: All apikit-owned commands have at minimum the 'auth' annotation key with value 'none', 'api_key', or 'admin'.
preconditions: full CLI command tree initialised
test_cases.input: walk all leaf commands (those with RunE) in the command tree
test_cases.expected: every leaf command has Annotations['auth'] in {'none', 'api_key', 'admin'}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: allLeaves = walkLeafCommands(rootCmd); for cmd in allLeaves: authVal = cmd.Annotations['auth']; assert authVal in ['none', 'api_key', 'admin']

test_cases.description: Child commands use PreRunE (not PersistentPreRunE); root's PersistentPreRunE runs automatically before child's PreRunE.
preconditions: a child command with PreRunE registered; PersistentPreRunE on root records invocation order
test_cases.input: invoke the child command
test_cases.expected: root's PersistentPreRunE runs before child's PreRunE; no PersistentPreRunE on child
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: order = []; rootCmd.PersistentPreRunE records 'root'; childCmd.PreRunE records 'child'; Execute(['childCmd']); assert order == ['root', 'child']; assert childCmd.PersistentPreRunE == nil

test_cases.description: PersistentPreRunE reads auth annotation from the cmd argument (leaf command), not the root command.
preconditions: a leaf command with 'auth': 'none' registered under root; root command has no 'auth' annotation
test_cases.input: invoke the leaf command; observe what PersistentPreRunE reads
test_cases.expected: cmd.Annotations['auth'] == 'none' inside PersistentPreRunE; root's annotations not consulted
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capturedAnnotations = nil; injectPersistentPreRunE(func(cmd, args){ capturedAnnotations = cmd.Annotations }); Execute(['authNoneCmd']); assert capturedAnnotations['auth'] == 'none'

test_cases.description: version_info.go declares Version, Build, TokenPrefix as package-level var with correct defaults; injectable via -ldflags.
preconditions: binary built without -ldflags overrides
test_cases.input: read cli.Version, cli.Build, cli.TokenPrefix
test_cases.expected: Version == 'dev'; Build == 'unknown'; TokenPrefix == 'ak'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert cli.Version == 'dev'; assert cli.Build == 'unknown'; assert cli.TokenPrefix == 'ak'

test_cases.description: TokenPrefix determines config directory path and is displayed in akc version output.
preconditions: TokenPrefix set to 'myapp' in test; HOME set to temp dir
test_cases.input: call InitConfig with TokenPrefix='myapp'; invoke 'akc version'
test_cases.expected: config directory is at $HOME/.myapp/; version output contains 'prefix': 'myapp'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cli.TokenPrefix = 'myapp'; configDir = homeDir + '/.myapp'; InitConfig(configDir); stdout = captureStdout(); Execute(['version']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.prefix == 'myapp'

test_cases.description: Root apikit package exports RootCommand() that delegates to internal/cli.RootCommand(); consuming projects can embed via AddCommand.
preconditions: root apikit package compiled
test_cases.input: consuming project calls apikit.RootCommand() and adds to its own root with AddCommand
test_cases.expected: returned command is non-nil; can be added via AddCommand; no internal/cli import needed
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: akcCmd = apikit.RootCommand(); assert akcCmd != nil; consumerRoot = newCobraCommand('myapp'); consumerRoot.AddCommand(akcCmd); assert findSubcommand(consumerRoot, 'akc') != nil

test_cases.description: internal/cli is declared as an internal package, preventing direct import from outside the module.
preconditions: Go toolchain available
test_cases.input: attempt to import github.com/txsvc/apikit/internal/cli from an external module
test_cases.expected: go build fails with 'use of internal package' error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = goCompile('package main; import _ "github.com/txsvc/apikit/internal/cli"'); assert result.exitCode != 0; assert contains(result.stderr, 'internal')

test_cases.description: CLI process exits with code 0 on success, 1 for *apikit.APIError, and 2 for all other errors.
preconditions: ExitCode function available
test_cases.input: call ExitCode with nil, *apikit.APIError, and plain error
test_cases.expected: 0, 1, 2 respectively
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert ExitCode(nil) == 0; assert ExitCode(newAPIError(403, 'forbidden')) == 1; assert ExitCode(errors.New('config missing')) == 2

test_cases.description: Error envelopes are written exclusively to stdout; stderr is empty for all error conditions.
preconditions: stdout and stderr captured; a command that produces an error is invoked
test_cases.input: invoke a command that returns a client error (missing endpoint_url)
test_cases.expected: stdout contains JSON error envelope; stderr is empty
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout, stderr = captureOutputs(); Execute(['someCmd']); assert isValidJSON(stdout); assert hasKey(parseJSON(stdout), 'error'); assert stderr == ''

test_cases.description: main.go produces no output; only calls os.Exit(cli.ExitCode(err)) after Execute() returns.
preconditions: cmd/akc/main.go source reviewed; Execute() returns without error
test_cases.input: inspect main.go source; Execute() returns nil
test_cases.expected: main.go contains no fmt.Print, os.Stdout.Write, or other output calls; only os.Exit(cli.ExitCode(err))
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mainSource = readFile('cmd/akc/main.go'); assert not contains(mainSource, 'fmt.Print'); assert not contains(mainSource, 'os.Stdout'); assert contains(mainSource, 'cli.ExitCode(err)'); assert contains(mainSource, 'os.Exit')

property_tests.description: For any error condition, PrintError writes exactly one valid JSON error envelope to stdout before Execute() returns, with nothing on stderr.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: arbitrary error values: *apikit.APIError with random code/message, plain errors with random message strings, wrapped errors
property_tests.invariant_check: stdout = captureStdout(); stderr = captureStderr(); Execute(cmdWithError); assert occurrences(stdout, '{"error":') == 1; assert isValidJSON(stdout); assert stderr == ''

property_tests.description: For any error value, ExitCode returns exactly 0, 1, or 2 consistently with the error type table.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: nil, *apikit.APIError with random HTTP status codes (400-599), plain errors, wrapped errors
property_tests.invariant_check: for nil: ExitCode(nil) == 0; for *apikit.APIError: ExitCode(err) == 1; for any other non-nil error: ExitCode(err) == 2; no other exit code value is ever returned

property_tests.description: For any combination of flag, env, and config values, ResolveField returns exactly one deterministic value per the precedence order with no ties.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: random combinations of (flagValue, flagChanged, envValue, configValue, required) covering all 2^3 source-set combinations
property_tests.invariant_check: val, err = ResolveField(field, flag, flagValue, flagChanged, env, configValue, required); if flagChanged and flagValue != '': assert val == flagValue and err == nil; else if envValue != '': assert val == envValue and err == nil; else if configValue != '': assert val == configValue and err == nil; else if required: assert val == '' and err != nil; else assert val == '' and err == nil

property_tests.description: For any call to SaveConfig, regardless of failure point, config.toml is never partially written and no temp files remain.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: random CLIConfig values; injected failures at each step (CreateTemp, chmod, close, Rename)
property_tests.invariant_check: originalContent = readFile(configPath) or ''; SaveConfig(configDir, cfg); // success or failure; configContent = readFile(configPath); assert isValidTOML(configContent) or configContent == originalContent; tempFiles = [f for f in listDir(configDir) if isTempFile(f)]; assert len(tempFiles) == 0

property_tests.description: For any command annotated with 'auth': 'none', PersistentPreRunE never loads config, resolves credentials, or constructs an apikit.Client.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: commands with 'auth': 'none' annotation and varying credential configurations (all missing, some present, all present)
property_tests.invariant_check: loadConfigCallCount = 0; resolveFieldCallCount = 0; newClientCallCount = 0; Execute(['authNoneCmd']); assert loadConfigCallCount == 0; assert resolveFieldCallCount == 0; assert newClientCallCount == 0

property_tests.description: For any command registered via AddCommand with at least one Annotations key, it appears in help --json output automatically without additional registration.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: random sets of commands with random Annotations maps (at least containing 'auth' key), registered via AddCommand
property_tests.invariant_check: for each testCmd in registeredAnnotatedCmds: rootCmd.AddCommand(testCmd); stdout = captureStdout(); Execute(['help', '--json']); cmds = parseJSON(stdout).commands; names = [c.name for c in cmds]; assert testCmd.CommandPath() in names

property_tests.description: For any value stored in context under any external key type, ClientFromContext and UserIDFromContext return only values stored under the unexported struct key types.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: contexts populated with *apikit.Client and string under clientContextKey{} plus arbitrary values under string keys, int keys, and other struct keys
property_tests.invariant_check: for each ctx in generatedContexts: client = ClientFromContext(ctx); uid = UserIDFromContext(ctx); if clientContextKey{} key was set to mockClient: assert client == mockClient; else assert client == nil; if userIDContextKey{} key was set to 'testUID': assert uid == 'testUID'; else assert uid == ''

edge_case_tests.description: Execute() calls PrintError exactly once when RunE returns an error; does not call PrintError when RunE returns nil.
preconditions: stub commands registered: one that returns error, one that returns nil
edge_case_tests.input: invoke error command; then invoke success command
edge_case_tests.expected: error invocation: stdout has exactly one error envelope; success invocation: stdout has no error envelope
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stdout1 = captureStdout(); Execute(['errorCmd']); count1 = occurrences(stdout1, '"error":'); assert count1 == 1; stdout2 = captureStdout(); Execute(['successCmd']); assert not contains(stdout2, '"error":')

edge_case_tests.description: When an unrecognized flag is passed, Cobra's error is intercepted; only the CLI's JSON error envelope appears on stdout; nothing on stderr.
preconditions: SilenceErrors and SilenceUsage set to true on root command
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains JSON error envelope with code:0; stderr is empty; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stdout, stderr = captureOutputs(); err = Execute(['--unknown-flag']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.error.code == 0; assert stderr == ''; assert ExitCode(err) == 2

edge_case_tests.description: When TokenPrefix is empty but the invoked command has 'auth': 'none', PersistentPreRunE skips TokenPrefix validation and returns nil.
preconditions: TokenPrefix set to empty string; a command with 'auth': 'none' annotation registered
edge_case_tests.expected: no TokenPrefix error; command proceeds normally; exit code 0
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: cli.TokenPrefix = ''; err = Execute(['authNoneCmd']); assert err == nil or ExitCode(err) == 0; assert not contains(capturedStdout, 'TokenPrefix is empty')

edge_case_tests.description: When $HOME is unresolvable, PersistentPreRunE exits with code 2 and error envelope with the exact message.
preconditions: os.UserHomeDir() mocked to return error; non-auth-exempt command registered
edge_case_tests.input: invoke non-auth-exempt command with HOME unresolvable
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains JSON envelope with code:0 and message 'cannot determine home directory: $HOME is not set or unresolvable'; exit code 2; no config init attempted
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: injectUserHomeDirError(); stdout = captureStdout(); err = Execute(['someCmd']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.error.code == 0; assert env.error.message == 'cannot determine home directory: $HOME is not set or unresolvable'; assert ExitCode(err) == 2

edge_case_tests.description: When config directory is unwritable and InitConfig fails, PersistentPreRunE propagates exit-2 error.
preconditions: HOME set to unwritable temp dir; non-auth-exempt command registered
edge_case_tests.input: invoke non-auth-exempt command with unwritable HOME
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains JSON error envelope with code:0; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: makeUnwritable(homeDir); stdout = captureStdout(); err = Execute(['someCmd']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.error.code == 0; assert ExitCode(err) == 2

edge_case_tests.description: LoadConfig rejects partially valid TOML (e.g., two valid fields and one invalid field), returning (nil, error) with no partial result.
preconditions: config.toml exists with two valid fields and one syntactically invalid field
edge_case_tests.input: call LoadConfig(configDir) with partially invalid TOML
edge_case_tests.expected: returns (nil, error); error message starts with 'config file is unparseable:'; no CLIConfig returned
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: writeFile(configPath, 'endpoint_url = "x"\nuser_id = "u"\napi_key = !!!BAD!!!'); cfg, err = LoadConfig(configDir); assert cfg == nil; assert err != nil; assert startsWith(err.Error(), 'config file is unparseable:')

edge_case_tests.description: ResolveField treats an empty string config value as unset, falling through to error (required=true) or ('', nil) (required=false).
preconditions: all env vars unset; config value is empty string
edge_case_tests.expected: returns ('', error) — empty config string does not count as valid resolved value
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: unsetEnv('ENDPOINT_URL'); val, err = ResolveField('endpoint_url', '--endpoint-url', '', false, 'ENDPOINT_URL', '', true); assert val == ''; assert err != nil; assert contains(err.Error(), 'endpoint_url is not set')

edge_case_tests.description: When leaf command has 'auth': 'none', PersistentPreRunE skips all credential resolution and client construction.
preconditions: no credentials configured anywhere; 'auth': 'none' command registered
edge_case_tests.input: invoke auth-exempt command with no credentials configured
edge_case_tests.expected: command runs without error; no config loading; no credential resolution; no client creation
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: unsetAllCredentials(); recordConfigLoadCalls = 0; err = Execute(['authNoneCmd']); assert err == nil or ExitCode(err) == 0; assert recordConfigLoadCalls == 0

edge_case_tests.description: PersistentPreRunE reads 'auth' annotation from the Cobra leaf command argument (cmd), not from the root command.
preconditions: leaf command with 'auth': 'none' registered; root command has no 'auth' annotation
edge_case_tests.expected: PersistentPreRunE's cmd argument has Annotations['auth'] == 'none'; root's Annotations is not consulted
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capturedCmd = nil; wrapPersistentPreRunE(func(cmd, args){ capturedCmd = cmd }); Execute(['authNoneLeaf']); assert capturedCmd.Annotations['auth'] == 'none'; assert capturedCmd.Use != 'akc'

edge_case_tests.description: Consuming project storing value under string key 'client' does not interfere with ClientFromContext returning the correct *apikit.Client.
preconditions: context has both a string-keyed 'client' value and a clientContextKey{}-keyed *apikit.Client
edge_case_tests.input: call ClientFromContext(ctx) where ctx has conflicting string key
edge_case_tests.expected: ClientFromContext returns the *apikit.Client stored under clientContextKey{}, not the string-keyed value
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockClient = newMockClient(); ctx = context.WithValue(context.Background(), 'client', 'impostor'); ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, clientContextKey{}, mockClient); result = ClientFromContext(ctx); assert result == mockClient; assert result != 'impostor'

edge_case_tests.description: Concurrent calls to SaveConfig result in last-writer-wins; config.toml is never in a partially written state.
preconditions: two goroutines call SaveConfig simultaneously with different CLIConfig values
edge_case_tests.expected: config.toml contains exactly one of the two updates; content is valid TOML; no temp files remain
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: go SaveConfig(configDir, cfg1); go SaveConfig(configDir, cfg2); wait(); content = readFile(configPath); assert isValidTOML(content); cfg = tomlDecode(content); assert cfg == cfg1 or cfg == cfg2; tempFiles = listDir(configDir); assert no temp file remains

edge_case_tests.description: PrintError with a plain (non-*apikit.APIError) error emits code:0 and never emits code:0 for *apikit.APIError errors.
preconditions: stdout captured
edge_case_tests.input: call PrintError with plain error; then with *apikit.APIError{Code: 500}
edge_case_tests.expected: plain error: envelope code is 0; *apikit.APIError: envelope code is 500 (not 0)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stdout1 = captureStdout(); PrintError(errors.New('oops')); assert parseJSON(stdout1).error.code == 0; stdout2 = captureStdout(); PrintError(newAPIError(500, 'server error')); assert parseJSON(stdout2).error.code == 500; assert parseJSON(stdout2).error.code != 0

edge_case_tests.description: Version command's context has a deadline/timeout; client.Version(ctx) call terminates within the deadline when server hangs.
preconditions: ENDPOINT_URL set to a server that never responds; context timeout injected
edge_case_tests.input: invoke 'akc version' with a hanging server
edge_case_tests.expected: command returns within the configured deadline; server_version is omitted; warning printed to stderr; exit code 0
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: setEnv('ENDPOINT_URL', 'http://hanging-server'); start = now(); stdout, stderr = captureOutputs(); err = Execute(['version']); elapsed = now() - start; assert elapsed < 10 seconds; env = parseJSON(stdout); assert not hasKey(env, 'server_version'); assert contains(stderr, 'warning'); assert ExitCode(err) == 0

edge_case_tests.description: akc help --json, akc help --json user, and akc help user --json all produce identical full JSON tree output.
preconditions: stub commands registered with Annotations; 'user' group command present
edge_case_tests.expected: all three stdout outputs are identical JSON
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: out1 = captureStdout(); Execute(['help', '--json']); out2 = captureStdout(); Execute(['help', '--json', 'user']); out3 = captureStdout(); Execute(['help', 'user', '--json']); assert out1 == out2; assert out2 == out3

edge_case_tests.description: A command with both RunE and child subcommands appears in help --json output as a leaf entry.
preconditions: a command with RunE and child subcommands registered; command has at least 'auth' annotation
edge_case_tests.expected: the dual-mode command appears in commands array as a leaf entry; child subcommands not affected
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: dualCmd = newCommand('dual', RunE, Annotations{'auth':'api_key'}); dualCmd.AddCommand(childCmd); rootCmd.AddCommand(dualCmd); Execute(['help', '--json']); cmds = parseJSON(stdout).commands; names = [c.name for c in cmds]; assert 'dual' in names

edge_case_tests.description: A child command defining PersistentPreRunE shadows the root's hook, breaking client initialization for its subcommands (forbidden pattern documented).
preconditions: a child command with PersistentPreRunE registered (test only to verify the pitfall)
edge_case_tests.input: invoke grandchild command under the misbehaving child
edge_case_tests.expected: root's PersistentPreRunE does NOT run; client is not initialized; this negative test confirms the forbidden pattern must not appear in real code
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: rootCalled = false; rootCmd.PersistentPreRunE records rootCalled = true; childWithBadHook.PersistentPreRunE = func(cmd,args){ return nil }; Execute(['childWithBadHook', 'grandchild']); assert rootCalled == false // demonstrates the shadow

edge_case_tests.description: When server and CLI are built with different TokenPrefix values, CLI reads from wrong config directory and produces missing-credential errors (no runtime check for mismatch).
preconditions: server compiled with TokenPrefix='srvprefix'; CLI compiled with TokenPrefix='cliprefix'; credentials in HOME/.srvprefix/config.toml only
edge_case_tests.input: invoke CLI command; CLI looks in HOME/.cliprefix/ which has no config
edge_case_tests.expected: exit code 2 with missing credential error envelope (no explicit prefix-mismatch error, just missing credential)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: cli.TokenPrefix = 'cliprefix'; writeConfig(homeDir+'/.srvprefix/config.toml', validConfig); stdout = captureStdout(); err = Execute(['someCmd']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.error.code == 0; assert contains(env.error.message, 'is not set'); assert ExitCode(err) == 2

edge_case_tests.description: When server returns 4xx/5xx, SDK wraps it as *apikit.APIError; PrintError uses HTTP status as code and server message; exit code 1.
preconditions: mock server configured to return 401 Unauthorized
edge_case_tests.input: invoke command against mock server; SDK returns *apikit.APIError{Code: 401, Message: 'unauthorized'}
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout envelope has code=401, message='unauthorized'; exit code 1; stderr empty
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stdout, stderr = captureOutputs(); err = Execute(['cmd']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.error.code == 401; assert env.error.message == 'unauthorized'; assert ExitCode(err) == 1; assert stderr == ''

edge_case_tests.description: Client-side error (missing endpoint_url) produces code:0 envelope on stdout; exit code 2; stderr empty.
preconditions: no endpoint_url configured anywhere; non-auth-exempt command; api_key configured to isolate the specific error
edge_case_tests.input: invoke non-auth-exempt command with no endpoint_url
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout envelope has code:0 and canonical message for endpoint_url; exit code 2; stderr empty
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: unsetEnv('ENDPOINT_URL'); setEnv('API_KEY', 'k'); stdout, stderr = captureOutputs(); err = Execute(['someCmd']); env = parseJSON(stdout); assert env.error.code == 0; assert contains(env.error.message, 'endpoint_url is not set'); assert ExitCode(err) == 2; assert stderr == ''

smoke_tests.description: Full end-to-end authenticated command: credentials resolved, client constructed, SDK called, JSON result printed, exit 0.
smoke_tests.trigger: invoke 'akc someCmd' with ENDPOINT_URL, API_KEY, USER_ID env vars set and a mock SDK server running
expected_effects: PersistentPreRunE runs: validates TokenPrefix, resolves HOME, creates config dir/file, loads config, resolves credentials, constructs client, stores in context
leaf command RunE calls ClientFromContext and invokes SDK method
PrintJSON writes indented JSON result to stdout

smoke_tests.description: API error from server: SDK returns *apikit.APIError, Execute() writes error envelope to stdout, process exits 1.
smoke_tests.trigger: invoke 'akc someCmd' with valid credentials; mock SDK returns *apikit.APIError{Code: 401, Message: 'unauthorized'}
expected_effects: leaf command RunE returns *apikit.APIError without printing

smoke_tests.description: Missing required credential: PersistentPreRunE returns canonical error; Execute() writes code:0 envelope to stdout; process exits 2.
smoke_tests.trigger: invoke 'akc someCmd' with no endpoint_url in any source; api_key configured
expected_effects: PersistentPreRunE reaches ResolveField for endpoint_url with all sources empty
ResolveField returns canonical error 'endpoint_url is not set: provide via --endpoint-url, $ENDPOINT_URL, or config file'
stdout contains '{"error":{"code":0,"message":"endpoint_url is not set: provide via --endpoint-url, $ENDPOINT_URL, or config file"}}'

smoke_tests.description: akc version with reachable server: auth-exempt path, ResolveEndpointURL, SDK Version() called, full JSON output with server_version.
smoke_tests.trigger: invoke 'akc version' with ENDPOINT_URL set to a running mock server
expected_effects: PersistentPreRunE reads 'auth':'none' and returns nil immediately
version RunE calls ResolveEndpointURL, gets non-empty URL
minimal apikit.Client constructed; Version(ctx) called with bounded context
VersionOutput built with ServerVersion from SDK response
PrintJSON writes JSON with cli_version, build, prefix, server_version to stdout

smoke_tests.description: akc help --json: full command tree walker runs, collects leaf commands, outputs JSON tree for agent discovery.
smoke_tests.trigger: invoke 'akc help --json' with stub command groups registered
mockable: stub 'user show' and 'admin users list' commands as test fixtures
expected_effects: only leaf commands with RunE and at least one Annotations key collected
JSON root object with name, version, commands written to stdout

smoke_tests.description: Atomic config write by a spec-14/15 command: temp file created in configDir, renamed atomically, no temp files left, config has correct content.
smoke_tests.trigger: a spec-14/15 stub command calls SaveConfig with updated CLIConfig after obtaining new credentials
expected_effects: CLIConfig marshalled to TOML via toml.NewEncoder
deferred os.Remove targets non-existent renamed path (no-op)
config.toml has updated content with mode 0600
no temp files remain in configDir
